name: basecamp.package2_dialog_rules_probe_mcw
description: Probe the current McWilliams ally dialogue/rules UI so Package 2 can restage a non-following assigned-camp hearer without guessing menu keys.

steps.label: wait_for_gameplay_settle
steps.expected_visible_fact: McWilliams gameplay is loaded with the nearby follower roster visible before dialogue input.
steps.failure_rule: If the loaded screen does not OCR a known nearby follower name, this scenario cannot prove follower dialogue/rules navigation.

steps.label: open_interaction_menu
steps.expected_visible_fact: The adjacent-creature interaction chooser is open.
expected_screen_text_after_contains: What do you want to do
steps.failure_rule: If the interaction chooser text is absent after pressing C, later talk/rules keys are wrong-screen evidence.

steps.label: choose_talk_to
steps.expected_visible_fact: The interaction-menu Talk to hotkey opens the creature talk selector.
abort_if_text_contains: What do you want to do
steps.abort_reason: still on the adjacent-creature interaction chooser after the talk-to key
steps.failure_rule: If the interaction chooser is still visible, stop instead of treating later keys as rules-dialogue proof.

steps.label: choose_first_talker
steps.expected_visible_fact: The first talker selection opens that NPC's dialogue surface.
abort_if_text_contains: What do you want to do
steps.abort_reason: still on a selector screen after choosing the first talker
steps.failure_rule: If the talker selector remains visible, do not treat later keys as follower dialogue/rules evidence.

steps.label: open_rules_window
steps.expected_visible_fact: The first follower dialogue response opens the follower rules UI.
abort_if_text_contains: What do you want to do
steps.abort_reason: still on a pre-dialogue selector after the supposed rules-window key
steps.failure_rule: If the rules UI text is absent, the scenario remains process-audit evidence only.
